kids_4_under.clue: The tradition of making promises to yourself on January 1st
kids_4_under.correctQuestion: What is a New Year's resolution?

kids_4_under.clue: Something you want to achieve or accomplish in the coming year
The first day of the year when most people promise to change their life
A fresh start or beginning that January symbolizes
The month when most New Year resolutions are made and broken
The process of planning what you want to accomplish
A list of things you want to do or achieve
The improvement of yourself through dedication and effort
Something you commit to doing regularly to reach your goals
The determination to see something through until completion

kids_10_under.clue: The practice of getting in shape and working out regularly
The most common New Year's resolution involving physical fitness
A promise to quit smoking, drinking, or other unhealthy habits
kids_10_under.correctQuestion: What is quitting a bad habit?

kids_10_under.clue: The goal to eat healthier and change your diet
Saving money for future security and opportunities
The aim to improve your skills or learn something new
A goal related to advancing in your career or job
The objective to spend more quality time with loved ones
The commitment to read more books throughout the year
A promise to be more organized and declutter your space

teenagers.clue: The aspiration to travel to new places and experience the world
A goal to improve your mental health and practice mindfulness
The objective to reduce stress and find inner peace

The goal to volunteer and help others in your community
A promise to be more confident and believe in yourself
The aim to repair or improve your relationships with others

The objective to be more responsible and dependable
